catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q1.explanation:
Statement:
“People had expected Andy Murray to become the world's top tennis player for at least five years before 2016.”

Đoạn:
“five years previously, he had been regarded as a talented outsider who entered but never won the major tournaments.”

Phân tích:

talented outsider ≠ expected future world number one
mọi người chưa thực sự tin anh sẽ thành số 1.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q2.explanation:
Statement:
“The change that Andy Murray made to his rackets attracted a lot of attention.”

Đoạn:
“Another change was so subtle as to pass more or less unnoticed.”

→ unnoticed = không được chú ý.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q3.explanation:
Statement:
“Most of the world's top players take a professional racket stringer on tour with them.”

Bài chỉ nhắc:
“Colin Triplow, a professional racket stringer”

Không có:

most players
take on tour

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q4.explanation:
Statement:
“Mike and Bob Bryan use rackets that are light”

Đoạn:
“The rackets they use now weigh more than the average model”

→ nặng hơn average.

→ FALSE.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q5.explanation:
Statement:
“Werner Fischer played with a spaghetti-strung racket that he designed himself.”

Bài chỉ nói:
“Werner Fischer started playing with the so-called spaghetti-strung racket.”

Không nói ông tự thiết kế.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q6.explanation:
Statement:
“The weather can affect how professional players adjust the strings”

Đoạn:
“They will continually change it depending on ... climatic conditions”

→ climatic conditions = weather.

→ TRUE.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q7.explanation:
Statement:
“It was believed that the change Pete Sampras made ... contributed to his strong serve.”

Đoạn:
“much of the serving power ... was attributed to the addition of four to five lead weights”

→ attributed to = believed caused by.

→ TRUE.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q8.explanation:
Câu:
“changes to the types of ___ used on their racket frames”

Đoạn:
“experimented with different kinds of paint”

→ Đáp án: paint.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q9.explanation:
Câu:
“because of the amount of ___ it created”

Đoạn:
“generated so much topspin”

→ Đáp án: topspin.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q10.explanation:
Câu:
“as important as players' diets or the ___ they do”

Đoạn:
“equal in significance to nutrition or training”

→ diets = nutrition.

→ Đáp án: training.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q11.explanation:
Câu:
“made from the ___ of animals”

Đoạn:
“made from the outer layer of sheep or cow intestines.”

→ Đáp án: intestines.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q12.explanation:
Câu:
“Pete Sampras had metal ___ put into the frames”

Đoạn:
“addition of four to five lead weights”

→ metal weights.

→ Đáp án: weights.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-1-q13.explanation:
Câu:
“Oliveira changed the ___ on his racket handles.”

Đoạn:
“replaced the original grips”

→ Đáp án: grips.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q14.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“a reference to a denial of involvement in piracy”

Đoạn D:
“the king of Alashiya ... rejected Akhenaten's claims of a connection with the Lukka”

→ phủ nhận liên quan tới piracy.

→ Đáp án: D.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q15.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“details of how a campaign to eradicate piracy was carried out”

Đoạn G:
“Pompey divided the entire Mediterranean into 13 districts...”

→ mô tả chi tiết chiến dịch chống piracy.

→ Đáp án: G.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q16.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“circumstances in which states ... would make use of pirates”

Đoạn C:
“during wartime, employing their skills and numbers against their opponents.”

→ states dùng pirates trong chiến tranh.

→ Đáp án: C.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q17.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“how people today commonly view pirates”

Đoạn A:
“an image springs to most people's minds...”

→ mô tả modern image of pirates.

→ Đáp án: A

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q18.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“how some people were encouraged not to return to piracy”

Đoạn G:
“many more were offered land in fertile areas located far from the sea.”

→ cho đất để họ trở thành farmers thay vì pirates.

→ Đáp án: G

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q19.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“need for many sailing vessels to stay relatively close to land”

Đoạn B:
“ships could not easily cross long distances over open water”

và:
“routes that followed the coastline.”

→ tàu phải đi gần coastline.

→ Đáp án: B.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q20.explanation:
Option B:
“They managed to escape capture ... because they knew the area so well.”

Đoạn B:
“knowledge of the local area helped the pirates to avoid retaliation”

→ đúng.

Option D:
“They depended more on the sea ... than on farming.”

Đoạn:
“relied heavily on marine resources”

→ đúng.

Các option khác:

stolen vessels → không có
paid for information → không có
stored goods in coves → không có

→ Đáp án:
B + D.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q21.explanation:
Option B:
“They managed to escape capture ... because they knew the area so well.”

Đoạn B:
“knowledge of the local area helped the pirates to avoid retaliation”

→ đúng.

Option D:
“They depended more on the sea ... than on farming.”

Đoạn:
“relied heavily on marine resources”

→ đúng.

Các option khác:

stolen vessels → không có
paid for information → không có
stored goods in coves → không có

→ Đáp án:
B + D.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q22.explanation:
Option C:
“Important officials were known to occasionally take part in piracy.”

Đoạn:
“Athenian ambassadors made a detour ... to capture a ship”

→ ambassadors = important officials.

→ đúng.

Option E:
“A favourable view of piracy is evident in certain ancient Greek texts.”

Đoạn:
“Homer not only condones but praises the lifestyle and actions of pirates.”

→ đúng.

→ Đáp án:
C + E.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q23.explanation:
Option C:
“Important officials were known to occasionally take part in piracy.”

Đoạn:
“Athenian ambassadors made a detour ... to capture a ship”

→ ambassadors = important officials.

→ đúng.

Option E:
“A favourable view of piracy is evident in certain ancient Greek texts.”

Đoạn:
“Homer not only condones but praises the lifestyle and actions of pirates.”

→ đúng.

→ Đáp án:
C + E.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q24.explanation:
grain

Câu:
“attacks on vessels transporting ___ to Rome”

Đoạn F:
“Pirate attacks on grain ships”

→ Đáp án: grain.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q25.explanation:
punishment

Câu:
“calls for ___ for the pirates responsible”

Đoạn:
“demanding punishment of the culprits.”

→ Đáp án: punishment.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-2-q26.explanation:
Câu:
“demanding a ___ for the return”

Đoạn:
“asking for a large ransom to be paid.”

→ Đáp án: ransom.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q27.explanation:
Question:
“What point does the writer make about misinformation in the first paragraph?”

Đoạn 1 nói:

people may spread misinformation to gain advantage
motivate others
protect relationships
accidentally share false information

→ có nhiều nguyên nhân khác nhau cho misinformation.

→ Đáp án D.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q28.explanation:
Question:
“What does the writer say about the role of technology?”

Đoạn 2:
“the means to correct misinformation might, over time, be found in those same patterns of mass communication”

→ technology có thể giúp tạo solution trong tương lai.

→ Đáp án A.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q29.explanation:
Question:
“What is the writer doing in the fourth paragraph?”

Đoạn 4:
“At least three observations ... warrant attention”

Sau đó liệt kê:

people initially believe misinformation
media doesn't block much misinformation
correcting misinformation is difficult

→ outlining important current issues.

→ Đáp án C.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q30.explanation:
Question:
“What point does the writer make about regulation in the USA?”

Đoạn:
“do not keep false advertising off the airwaves.”

→ regulation không ngăn misinformation xuất hiện.

→ Đáp án D.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q31.explanation:
H — been exposed

Câu:
“Although people have ___ to misinformation”

Đoạn:
“it is fairly routine for individuals to come across information that is false”

→ come across = been exposed to.

→ H.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q32.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Descartes, Spinoza, opinions, theory

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Paragraph 6

Bước 3: Phân tích

Hai triết gia có quan điểm khác nhau về cách con người xử lý thông tin
→ different ideas

Đáp án
different ideas

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q33.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

mental process, labeling truth, stage

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Paragraph 6

Bước 3: Phân tích

Spinoza cho rằng có một quá trình riêng biệt để xác minh đúng sai sau khi tiếp nhận thông tin
→ mental operation

Đáp án
mental operation

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q34.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

research, support theory, evidence

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Paragraph 6

Bước 3: Phân tích

Nghiên cứu hiện đại đã xác nhận quan điểm của Spinoza
→ additional evidence

Đáp án
additional evidence

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q35.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

short time, accept information, moment

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Paragraph 6

Bước 3: Phân tích

Con người coi thông tin là đúng chỉ trong một thời gian rất ngắn trước khi kiểm tra lại
→ short period

Đáp án
short period

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q36.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

brain, scepticism, perception, location

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Paragraph 6

Bước 3: Phân tích

Khả năng nghi ngờ và khả năng nhận thức nằm ở các vùng khác nhau của não
→ different locations

Đáp án
different locations

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q37.explanation:
Statement:
“Campaigns ... will fail if people are unable to understand them.”

Đoạn:
“For corrective campaigns to be persuasive, audiences need to be able to comprehend them”

→ nếu không hiểu → không persuasive.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q38.explanation:
Statement:
“Attempts to teach elementary school students ... have been opposed.”

Bài chỉ nói:
“suggests the utility of media literacy efforts as early as elementary school.”

Không nói:

bị phản đối.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q39.explanation:
Statement:
“It may be possible to overcome misinformation in a relatively short period.”

Đoạn cuối:
“The journey forward undoubtedly will be a long and arduous one.”

→ ngược hoàn toàn với “short period”.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-19-1-reading-part-3-q40.explanation:
Statement:
“The need to keep up with new information is hugely exaggerated today.”

Không có ý nào như vậy trong bài.

→ NOT GIVEN.
